title: Arteris at DAC 2026 Connecting Innovation for Silicon Success - Semiwiki
gist: Primarily an Arteris DAC 2026 booth promo (network-on-chip interconnect IP with 950+ design starts and 4 billion devices shipped), but the article body also embeds a separate item reporting Apple's $30B+, multi-year (through 2031) chip-supply deal with Broadcom for US-made RF/wireless components, under which Broadcom will invest $1.5 billion to expand its Fort Collins, Colorado facility to produce at least 15 billion chips.

insights.claim: Arteris' network-on-chip interconnect, system integration, and hardware security IP has been used in more than 950 design starts and shipped in 4 billion devices, positioning the company as infrastructure for AI, hyperscale data center, automotive, industrial, and chiplet/multi-die designs.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: 950+ design starts and 4B shipped devices is a scale benchmark for Arteris' NoC IP penetration, relevant when assessing Arteris' competitive position in the growing chiplet/3D-IC interconnect IP market.
insights.quote: More than 950 design starts and 4 billion devices shipped demonstrate the industry's confidence in Arteris technology and expertise.

themes.theme: Apple-Broadcom US RF supply deal (embedded item)

insights.claim: Apple's planned $30 billion-plus, multi-year chip-supply agreement with Broadcom extends through 2031 and centers on U.S.-made wireless and radio-frequency components, including FBAR filters supporting 5G, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and GPS connectivity in Apple devices.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A $30B+ multi-year Apple-Broadcom RF supply lock-in through 2031 is a major revenue visibility event for Broadcom and a concrete step in Apple's US semiconductor sourcing diversification, relevant to both companies' supply-chain risk profiles.
insights.quote: the multi-year deal extends through 2031 and centers on U.S.-made wireless and radio-frequency components, including FBAR filters used to support connectivity functions in Apple devices such as 5G, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth and GPS

insights.claim: Broadcom will invest about $1.5 billion to expand its Fort Collins, Colorado facility as part of the Apple deal, with the program expected to produce at least 15 billion chips.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A concrete $1.5B domestic capacity investment tied to a specific 15-billion-chip production commitment is a quantifiable US semiconductor onshoring data point relevant to CHIPS Act-era manufacturing investment tracking.
insights.quote: Broadcom will invest about $1.5 billion to expand its Fort Collins, Colorado facility, with the program expected to produce at least 15 billion chips.

insights.claim: The Apple-Broadcom RF deal is framed as both a supply-chain resilience move and a policy-aligned domestic manufacturing investment, since Apple's most advanced application processors and memory remain tied to global foundries and suppliers while RF chips are a more practical category for U.S. capacity expansion given their specialized, long-qualification-cycle manufacturing processes.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Clarifies that Apple's US reshoring push is concentrated in RF/connectivity components rather than its most advanced application processors or memory, tempering expectations that this deal signals broader US onshoring of Apple's core silicon.
insights.quote: Although Apple's most advanced application processors and memory components remain tied to global foundries and suppliers, RF chips are a practical category for U.S. capacity expansion because they rely on specialized manufacturing processes and long-term qualification cycles.
